publicationStmt.publisher: "Maryland, Register of Wills Records, 1629-1999." Images. FamilySearch. http://FamilySearch.org: 3 December 2024. Citing Prerogative Court. Hall of Records, Annapolis.

div.head: Peter Carr. Will
div.p:
In the name of God Amen I Peter Carr of Charles County in the provence of
Maryland Planter lying such and weake in body but sound and perfect memory
to make and ordain this my last will and testament in manner and form
following -- First I give and bequeath my soule unto the hands of
almighty God believing that through the Death and passion of his dear son &
my blessed savious Jesus Christ I may be made an inheriter of Eternal life and
my
my body I commit to the ground to be interned in such decent manner as
my Executor hereafter named shall think -- and as to the Dispersal of
the Estate that God gath veen pleased (in this world) to give me I give
and disperse the same as followeth v-- Item after my debts and personal
Expenses paid & discharged I give and bequeath unto Robert Charles Joseph
Elizabeth and Sarah Douglas sons and daughters of Col. John Douglas late
of Charles County deceased. the sum and quantity of ten thousand seven hundred
thiry nine pounds of tobacco to be equally divided amonst them by even
portions when they shall arrive to the age of sixteene years the said sum of
tobacco being due to me from Sarah Douglas now wife of Robert Smith and
mother of said Leg--as appears by a bill under her hands and seal
bearing date the ninth day of May 1679.

Item. I give Peter Mackmillion son of George Mackmillion late of Charles
County deceased six thousand pounds of tobacco and one Roane filly
shall come to the afe of sixteen years and in the meane tyme the said Peter to
remaine in the custody & serve the remainer of his time with Joseph Buttell
and the said Peter shall arrive to the age aforesaid.

Item After all the debts and Leg-- paid all the -- and remainder of
my Estate both real and personall I give and bequeath as foll. I give unto Jane Smith wife of John Smith my servant one half thereof together with their freedom
one fourth part thereof unto James Tyler son of James Tyler my Exectuor
hereafter named and the remaining fourth part unto children od George Shane and those alike and I do hereby nominate and appoint my loving
James Tyler of Charles County
Executor this my last will and
testiment
and request him to -- the same Executed In Testimony
whereof I have hereunto set my hand and seal this 21st day of September
Anno
Anno Domino 1680

Signed Sealed published & Decleared by
the above named Peter Carr at his last
will and Testamant in the presence of us
William 12 Philmore:-John Telby Ed Dennis
his mouth his mark
the mark of
Peter M Carr

On the back of the foregoing will was endorsed the following Probate (to will)
April 19th 1683. THis day Came Mr. Philmore and Edmond Dennis two of the
witnesses of the written will before me Humphrey Narner one of his Lordships
Justice of the Peace for Charles County and made oath that the written
named Peter Carr did sign seal and Deliver the within written as his o--
and deed and did declare the same to be his last will and testament
3 3/4 Sides
Hum. Narner
